title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot addresses the gathered crowd at the Blore House Hotel and calls upon Nancy Duane, a portrait painter, to assist in his storytelling. Wearing a scarf to hide her face, Nancy recounts her forbidden love for the late Patrick Ives. She describes how Patrick claimed to communicate with the deceased for financial gain and details her confession of their affair at the King's Head Inn. She mentions the "poisoned" deaths of Patrick and Frances Ives but omits the name of Jenny Hobbs. Poirot comforts Nancy by revealing that Patrick had written her a letter of love before he died.

Poirot then calls upon Jenny Hobbs, Patrick’s former maid. Jenny confesses that her jealousy of Nancy led her to spread the rumors that ruined Patrick sixteen years prior. She admits to conspiring with three accomplices—Harriet Sipper, Richard Nigges, and Ada Gransberry—to make amends for their past actions. Jenny explains their plan was to frame Nancy Duane for the deaths of the three accomplices. Henry Nigges testifies that his brother Richard is not the same man he once knew, leaving Richard's specific involvement ambiguous.

Poirot concludes that there was no murderer; instead, the deaths were a series of suicides and killings permitted by the participants. He identifies Jenny Hobbs and Samuel Kid as criminals for conspiring to frame Nancy. Under questioning, Kid admits to placing a specific note at the Brooks Han hotel reception desk as instructed by Jenny. When questioned how he obtained the room keys to plant on Nancy, Kid claims he borrowed a master key from an unnamed male hotel employee, though he cannot identify the man.
